meta.title: Jaguar 306DT / 306PS Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.h1: Jaguar 306DT / 306PS Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Jaguar 306DT is a 3.0-litre V6 diesel, the 306PS a petrol supercharged version — both face similar reliability concerns with the V6 architecture. Backed by 980+ real UK enquiries in 2025 [EM-VERIFIED].

rows.value: Also fitted to Land Rover models

verdictRating.confidence: 88% confidence — based on 980 UK enquiries and cross-referenced specialist rebuild reports [EM-VERIFIED].

rows.score: 8 (Midpoint of Reconditioned (Supply Only) range: £6,500–£9,500 → midpoint £8,000 → falls in £7,000–£9,999 bracket → 8/20.)
4 (Catastrophic bearing failure leaves little rebuildable material — failures are typically all-or-nothing, full-replacement events.)
20 (Well-supported parts network; common across both Jaguar and Land Rover applications.)
4 (Reconditioned cost (£6,500–£9,500) against conservative lowest host value (XF X250 at £4,000–£12,000, worst-case £4,000) = 163%+ — over threshold.)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: "The 306DT and 306PS are high-performance V6s with a known fatal flaw — crankshaft bearing failure can strike without warning from 80,000 miles — budget for a full replacement, not a rebuild."
verdictRating.bestFor: Low-mileage, well-maintained cars with full specialist history.
verdictRating.avoidIf: No documented oil-change history or any cold-start knock present.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Also powers the Land Rover Range Rover, Sport, Discovery and Velar → [link to Land Rover 306DT page].

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting a 306DT typically takes 15–20 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [EM-QUOTE].
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Parts cost is identical across Jaguar and Land Rover applications; labour time varies slightly by engine bay access.

cta.label: → Compare 306DT / 306PS engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Cost — "How much does a Jaguar 306DT engine replacement cost?"
items.answer: A reconditioned 306DT typically costs £6,500–£9,500 supply only [EM-QUOTE], varying by specific variant and output. Fitting adds £975–£1,300 depending on labour rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

items.question: Reliability — "Is the Jaguar 306DT a reliable engine?"
items.answer: We rate the 306DT at 2.5★ out of 5 based on 980+ UK enquiries [EM-VERIFIED]. While many examples exceed 120,000 miles without issue, the crankshaft bearing design is a known weak point that demands preventative maintenance.

items.question: Common failure — "What is the most common Jaguar 306DT failure?"
items.answer: Crankshaft bearing failure (tin-aluminium coating fatigue) is the defining failure, typically striking from 80,000–140,000 miles. Cold-start knock is the early warning sign; catastrophic failure follows without intervention.

items.question: Fitment — "Which Jaguar models use the 306DT and 306PS?"
items.answer: The XF (both X250 and X260), XJ X351, F-Pace and the F-Type all use the 306DT diesel or 306PS petrol V6 across 2009–2020 — five model lines spanning both saloon and SUV applications.

items.question: Shared mode — "Is the Jaguar 306DT the same as Land Rover's version?"
items.answer: Yes — the 306DT and 306PS are the same physical engine across Jaguar and Land Rover applications, shared under the AJD V6 architecture. Parts are identical; only ECU tuning and fitment specifics vary between brands.
